steps.title: Average requires only sum and count
steps.content:
The average (or mean) is the total sum divided by the number of items. We have been given exactly what we need:
$$\text{Average} = \frac{\text{Sum}}{\text{Count}} = \frac{96}{12} = 8\text{ books per volunteer}$$
This calculation uses only the total (96) and count (12), both provided in the problem.

steps.title: Other measures require individual data we do not have
steps.content:
The median, maximum, and minimum each require knowing the individual totals for each volunteer, information we lack. Consider:
  • Median: To find the median of 12 values, we must arrange them in order and locate the middle value. We cannot determine this from the sum alone.
  • Maximum: Without knowing each volunteer's total, the maximum could range from 8 books (if evenly distributed) to 96 books (if one volunteer collected all). It is indeterminate.
  • Minimum: Likewise indeterminate without individual values, could be 0 or any number up to 8.
Only the average can be calculated from the sum and count. That confirms A is correct.
